summary.objective: To introduce and analyze a new paradigm of 'group chase and escape' where one group of chasers pursues another group of escapees using a simple model.
summary.methods: Simulations on a two-dimensional lattice with hopping rules: targets evade their nearest chaser, and chasers close in on their nearest target; extensions include search range limitations and hopping fluctuations.
summary.results: Revealed an optimal number of chasers that minimizes the cost per target, observed self-organized spatial patterns, and found that certain fluctuations reduce catch times, similar to stochastic resonance.
summary.conclusion: The model exhibits complex behavior distinct from reaction-diffusion systems, providing a basis for studying collective pursuit-evasion in multi-agent systems and swarm intelligence.
